title: Edit-time nudge surfacing: file-context fires a file's watcher nudge at the PreToolUse moment
summary: Watcher nudges now surface at the edit moment, not just the next prompt. surfacePendingNudges mcp/kernel.ts gained an optional opts.path filter: with a path it surfaces ONLY nudges whose .file matches that path normalize
body:
Watcher nudges now surface at the edit moment, not just the next prompt. surfacePendingNudges (mcp/kernel.ts) gained an optional opts.path filter: with a path it surfaces ONLY nudges whose .file matches that path (normalized via the shared normalizeRepoPath helper) and marks just those surfaced; with no path (prompt-context / UserPromptSubmit) it drains every pending nudge. The file-context CLI command (mcp/cli.ts) — the target of the PreToolUse Edit/Read hooks (kage file-context --path <file>) — now calls surfacePendingNudges(project, { path }) and appends the nudge block to its output, so the UNCHANGED shell hooks surface it (a nudge-only output is non-empty and gets injected via additionalContext). Net: a file-scoped nudge fires the instant that file is touched (highest-value trigger), a file-less nudge waits for the next prompt, each nudge shows exactly once. normalizeRepoPath unifies path matching with kageFileContext citation lookup (also strips trailing slashes); the nudge inbox is written atomically (tmp + rename) because file-context now rewrites it per-edit. The MCP server (mcp/index.ts) does NOT expose file-context — surfacing is purely the CLI hook path, so no MCP change was needed.
Verified by: npm test (354 unit + 12 dogfood pass); end-to-end through built + global 2.5.3 CLI (file-context --path surfaces only the matching file's nudge, once; prompt-context drains the rest); 3-lens adversarial workflow review (all verdicts ship)
